title: Investigate: Quick Win Features (sound, corpse looting, revival shrines)
description:
Small-effort features to investigate and prototype:

1. **Sound Effects & Combat Audio** — Retro 8-bit sounds via Web Audio API for abilities, hits, deaths, pickups. Touches: canvas, CombatServer, LootServer.

2. **Persistent Corpse Looting** — Dead NPCs leave searchable corpses for 30s with bonus gold/items, creating risk/reward mid-combat. Touches: LootServer, AIServer, Mnesia, canvas.

3. **Revival Shrines** — One-time resurrection points in dungeons that consume gold. Touches: DungeonServer, PartyServer, LootServer, canvas.

title: Class selection and character creation UI on /play
description: Currently GameLive hard-codes class to warrior and generates a random player_id. Add a pre-game screen at /play that lets players: (1) Enter a character name, (2) Choose a class (warrior/mage/ranger/rogue) with stat previews from Classes module, (3) Pick a dungeon (crypt_of_bones or spider_den) with difficulty selector. Implementation: Add a :lobby phase to GameLive assigns. Render an HTML form (LiveView, no canvas needed) when phase is :lobby. On submit, call spawn_player with chosen class and create_instance with chosen dungeon. Transition to :playing phase which shows the canvas. This is the single highest-impact change because right now every player is a warrior in crypt_of_bones with no choice.

title: Investigate: Dungeon & World Systems (events, objects, threat, spectator)
description:
Medium-effort features for richer dungeon gameplay:

1. **Dungeon Events & Ambushes** — Random timed events: sudden spawns, treasure goblins, merchants, collapsing walls. Touches: DungeonServer, GameLoop, AIServer, canvas.

2. **Interactive Objects (Doors, Chests, Levers)** — Proximity-activated objects that unlock areas or spawn loot/enemies. Touches: DungeonServer, Movement, canvas, Mnesia.

3. **Threat/Aggro System** — NPCs target based on damage/healing/taunts; tanks can manage enemy focus. Touches: AIServer, CombatServer, canvas.

4. **Spectator Mode** — Watch active dungeon runs in real-time via separate LiveView. Touches: Phoenix Channels, canvas, PartyServer.

5. **Leaderboards & Run Metrics** — Track fastest clears, highest damage, survival streaks. Touches: SurrealDB, new MetricsServer, LiveView.

title: Investigate: Big-Ticket Features (boss phases, procgen dungeons, skill trees, mutators)
description:
Large-effort, high-impact features to explore:

1. **Boss Encounters with Phases** — Elites change behavior/abilities at HP thresholds, announce phases, guaranteed rare loot. Touches: AIServer, CombatServer, LootServer, canvas.

2. **Procedural Dungeon Layouts** — BSP or cellular automata room generation, different each run. Touches: DungeonServer, new MapGenerator, canvas.

3. **Skill Trees & Build Customization** — Unlock passives and ability mods between runs (crit chance, CDR, AoE radius). Touches: new ProgressionServer, SurrealDB, CombatServer.

4. **Seasonal Modifiers & Mutators** — Weekly rotating gameplay modifiers (double gold, enraged enemies, mirrored controls). Touches: GameLoop, CombatServer, Movement, new ModifierServer, canvas.

title: Research native/custom rendering alternatives to LiveView
description: Research and compare alternatives to LiveView+Canvas for the game frontend. Investigate: (1) Scenic - Elixir native UI framework, (2) Rayex (github.com/shiryel/rayex) - Raylib bindings for Elixir, (3) Any other Elixir NIFs or ports for game rendering (SDL2, OpenGL bindings, etc.), (4) Nerves-based or desktop Elixir approaches. For each option evaluate: maturity/maintenance status, rendering performance, input handling, how it integrates with our OTP game engine, installation complexity, and provide a concrete example of how the game loop would connect to the renderer. Produce a comparison document.

title: Create GameLive LiveView + Canvas JS hook
description: Build a Phoenix LiveView at /play that: (1) spawns a player character on mount, (2) subscribes to PubSub game state, (3) handles phx-window-keydown/keyup for WASD movement + ability keys, (4) pushes state to a JS canvas hook for rendering. The JS hook draws players as colored rectangles, NPCs as red shapes, loot as yellow dots. Include basic HUD (HP/mana bars, ability cooldowns). Auto-spawns a dungeon instance with monsters so there's something to fight.

title: Wire PubSub broadcasts in GameLoop
description: Uncomment/implement the broadcast call in GameLoop so game state is pushed via Phoenix.PubSub every tick. Broadcast player positions, NPC state, loot piles, and combat events. This is the foundation for any realtime frontend.

title: Account registration and login flow with SurrealDB
description: SurrealDB has accounts and characters tables but no registration or login flow exists. Currently /play auto-spawns an anonymous player. Add: (1) A simple registration form (username + password) at /play before the lobby. Use Bcrypt to hash passwords. Write to SurrealDB accounts table. (2) A login form that queries SurrealDB for matching account, verifies password hash, stores account_id in Phoenix session. (3) On successful login, query characters table for existing character. If found, pre-populate the class selection. If not, show character creation. (4) Module: lib/slow_arena/accounts.ex with register/2, login/2, get_character/1. This bridges the gap between the SurrealDB schema (which already defines accounts/characters) and the actual game client which currently ignores persistence entirely. Depends on: character persistence issue (slow-arena-wr1) which is in-progress.

title: Death screen overlay and dungeon restart flow
description: When a player dies, respawn happens silently after 5s with no feedback. Add: (1) A death overlay on the canvas showing 'YOU DIED' with a respawn countdown timer (5s), drawn in game_canvas.js when localPlayer.dead is true. (2) A 'dungeon complete' screen when all NPCs are dead, showing kill count, gold earned, items collected, and a 'Run Again' or 'Return to Lobby' button. (3) Wire the 'Run Again' button to DungeonServer.cleanup_instance + create_instance to reset the dungeon. (4) Wire 'Return to Lobby' to transition GameLive back to :lobby phase (from issue above). The broadcast already sends player.dead boolean. The JS canvas just needs to check it and render an overlay. This makes the game loop feel complete instead of just silently respawning.

title: Investigate: Visual & Combat Polish (hazards, minimap, buffs/debuffs, combos)
description:
Medium-effort features focused on visual feedback and combat depth:

1. **Environmental Hazards & Traps** — Spike traps, fire pits, poison vents as dynamic dungeon tiles. Touches: DungeonServer, CombatServer, Movement, canvas.

2. **Minimap with Fog of War** — Real-time minimap revealing explored areas, party members, enemies, loot. Touches: DungeonServer, canvas, Mnesia.

3. **Buff/Debuff Visual Effects** — Colored auras, particles, icons for status effects (poison, shield, etc). Touches: CombatServer, canvas.

4. **Combo System & Ability Synergies** — Party ability chains trigger bonus effects (freeze + shatter = 2x). Touches: CombatServer, PartyServer, canvas.

title: Wire SurrealDB client module using Req + HTTP API
description:
Create a thin SlowArena.SurrealDB module wrapping Req to talk to SurrealDB's /sql endpoint.

**Approach:** Use Req (add to mix.exs deps) with HTTP POST to localhost:8000/sql. No heavy client library needed — SurrealDB's HTTP API is simple.

**Module: lib/slow_arena/surreal_db.ex**
- query(sql) -> {:ok, results} | {:error, reason}
- query(sql, params) -> parameterized queries
- Config from runtime.exs: host, port, namespace, database, user, pass
- Auth header: Basic base64(user:pass), NS/DB headers
- Connection pooling via Req/Finch (already in Phoenix deps)

**Config (config/runtime.exs):**
  config :slow_arena, :surrealdb,
    url: System.get_env("SURREALDB_URL", "http://localhost:8000"),
    namespace: "slow_arena",
    database: "game",
    username: "root",
    password: "root"

**Tasks:**
1. Add {:req, "~> 0.5"} to mix.exs
2. Create SlowArena.SurrealDB module with query/1, query/2
3. Add config to runtime.exs
4. Add to application supervisor (optional process for connection health)
5. Test with: SlowArena.SurrealDB.query("INFO FOR DB")
6. Create priv/seeds/seed.exs that defines all tables
close_reason: SurrealDB client module wired: query/1, query!/1, healthy?/0 working with Req HTTP client

title: Run history and leaderboards
description:
Write dungeon run results to SurrealDB when a run completes, and expose leaderboard queries.

**On dungeon complete (DungeonServer or GameLive):**
- Detect boss_defeated or all NPCs dead
- Collect metrics from combat_events in Mnesia: npcs_killed, total damage dealt/taken
- Calculate duration from instance created_at
- Write run_history record to SurrealDB with rewards (gold, items, XP)
- Award XP to character, level up if threshold met

**Leaderboard queries:**
- Fastest completion per dungeon+difficulty
- Most NPCs killed in a single run
- Highest damage dealt
- Expose via LiveView page or CLI command

**CLI additions:**
- leaderboard <dungeon> — show top 10 fastest clears
- runs <player> — show recent run history

Depends on: SurrealDB client module, schema seed, character persistence.

title: Inventory UI panel and health potion usage
description: Items are collected into Mnesia player_inventory but there is no way to view or use them. Add: (1) An inventory panel toggled by pressing 'I' or 'Tab', rendered as a canvas overlay in game_canvas.js showing item names, quantities, and rarity colors. (2) Health potion consumption: press a key (e.g. 'Q') to use a health_potion from inventory, restoring 50 HP. Decrement quantity in player_inventory, delete entry if 0. (3) Include the inventory data in the broadcast by reading player_inventory in Broadcast.collect_players for the local player. (4) Show equipped weapon name in the HUD. This makes the loot system meaningful -- right now players collect items that they can never see or use. Touches: Broadcast (add inventory to player data), GameLive (new keydown handler for Q and I/Tab), game_canvas.js (inventory overlay + potion use feedback), CombatServer or new module for consumable effects.

title: Character persistence: save/load between sessions
description:
Wire character save/load so progress persists across browser sessions.

**On login (GameLive.mount):**
- If account has a character in SurrealDB, load it instead of spawning fresh
- Restore: class, level, stats, gold, position, inventory
- Write loaded data into Mnesia tables (player_stats, player_positions, player_gold, player_inventory, player_equipment)
- If no character exists, create one in SurrealDB after spawning in Mnesia

**Periodic save (every 30s or on significant events):**
- Read current state from Mnesia
- UPDATE character in SurrealDB: position, gold, stats (hp regen may have changed max values at higher levels)
- Upsert inventory rows

**On logout (GameLive.terminate):**
- Final save to SurrealDB before cleanup_player wipes Mnesia
- Update last_played timestamp

**Sync strategy:**
- Mnesia is always the source of truth during gameplay (10Hz reads)
- SurrealDB is the source of truth between sessions (loaded on mount)
- No SurrealDB reads during gameplay — only writes on save checkpoints

**New module: lib/slow_arena/persistence.ex**
- save_character(player_id) — Mnesia -> SurrealDB
- load_character(player_id) — SurrealDB -> Mnesia
- create_character(player_id, class) — create in both
close_reason: Persistence module complete: create_character, load_character, save_character. 30s periodic save + save on disconnect. Idempotent seed script.

title: Define SurrealDB schema and seed script
description:
Create the persistent schema in SurrealDB via a seed script at priv/seeds/seed.exs.

**6 tables:**

1. **accounts** — SCHEMAFULL. Fields: username (string, unique), password_hash, email, created_at, last_login. Index on username.

2. **characters** — SCHEMAFULL. Fields: account (record<accounts>), name (unique), class (warrior/mage/ranger/rogue), level, experience, gold, stats (object: max_hp, max_mana, str, int, agi, armor), position (object: x, y, zone, facing), created_at, last_played. Index on account, name.

3. **item_definitions** — SCHEMAFULL. Master item catalog. Fields: item_id (unique), name, description, type (weapon/armor/accessory/consumable/material), rarity (common/uncommon/rare/epic/legendary), level_requirement, slot, stats (object), stack_size, vendor_price. Seed with existing loot table items: health_potion, goblin_ear, bone_fragment, rusty_sword, ogre_club, rare_gem, ectoplasm, spirit_shard, spider_silk, venom_sac, dark_tome, bone_staff, slime_gel.

4. **inventory** — SCHEMAFULL. Fields: character (record<characters>), item (record<item_definitions>), quantity, equipped (bool), acquired_at. Unique index on (character, item).

5. **dungeon_templates** — SCHEMAFULL. Fields: template_id (unique), name, description, min_level, max_level, difficulty (object: hp/damage/xp/loot multipliers), npc_spawns (array of objects), loot_tables (array), map (object: width, height). Seed with crypt_of_bones template.

6. **run_history** — SCHEMAFULL. Fields: character (record), dungeon (record), party_members, started_at, completed_at, duration_seconds, result (completed/failed/abandoned), metrics (object: npcs_killed, damage_dealt, damage_taken, deaths), rewards (object: xp, gold, items).

**Seed script:** mix run priv/seeds/seed.exs — calls SlowArena.SurrealDB.query() to define all tables and insert item_definitions + dungeon_templates.
close_reason: 6 SCHEMAFULL tables defined, 13 items + 2 dungeon templates seeded. Fixed SurrealDB v2 headers (surreal-ns/surreal-db). Seed script at priv/seeds/seed.exs.

title: Class-specific ability loadouts and ability bar UI
description: All 6 abilities are available to every class, but classes have different stat scalings (warrior=str, mage=int, ranger/rogue=agi). Add class-specific ability loadouts so each class gets 3-4 abilities that match their identity. Implementation: (1) Add an abilities field to Classes.stats/1 returning a list of ability_ids per class, e.g. warrior: [slash, shield_bash], mage: [fireball, ice_lance], ranger: [arrow_volley, slash], rogue: [backstab, slash]. (2) In GameLive, filter @ability_keys to only the abilities for the player's class. (3) Update the canvas ability bar hint to show only available abilities with correct key bindings. (4) In CombatServer.do_cast_ability, add a check that the character's class can use the requested ability. This makes classes feel distinct and meaningful rather than everyone having the same 6 buttons.

title: Dungeon completion detection and XP/leveling system
description: There is no detection for when a dungeon run is finished, and no XP or leveling. Add: (1) Dungeon completion check in GameLoop tick: when all NPCs for an instance_id are dead, mark dungeon_instances status as :completed. Broadcast a :dungeon_complete event. (2) XP awards: each NPC kill grants XP based on template (goblin=10, skeleton=15, spider=8, ghost=12, slime=5, necromancer=50, boss_ogre=75). Track XP in a new Mnesia table or add to player_stats. (3) Level-up thresholds: level N requires N*100 XP. On level up, increase max_hp by 10, max_mana by 5, and primary stat by 2 based on class. Broadcast a :level_up event for the canvas to show a celebration effect. (4) Show level and XP bar in the HUD. This creates progression, a core gameplay loop, and feeds into the character persistence system (slow-arena-wr1). Touches: GameLoop, CombatServer.handle_npc_death, Broadcast, game_canvas.js HUD, Classes module (for per-level stat growth).
